In January 1995, Louisiana State Police homicide detectives Rustin "Rust" Cohle and Martin "Marty" Hart arrive at a crime scene in the cane fields outside Erath, Louisiana. They discover the body of Dora Kelly Lange, a young woman and former prostitute, posed in a prayer position and tied to a tree trunk. She wears a crown of foliage and deer antlers, her back is painted with a blue spiral symbol, and small twig lattice structures surround her. The victim was drugged, bound, tortured, strangled, and then carefully posed; her body was washed clean, leaving no fingerprints.

The detectives learn that Dora recently called her ex-husband Charlie Lange from prison, telling him she would "become a nun" and that she had "met a king." Their investigation also surfaces the case of Marie Fontenot, a young girl who disappeared five years earlier under suspicious circumstances — the case was never properly investigated. Mysterious "devil nets" (stick lattice structures) were found on property connected to her disappearance. Another report mentions a child who claimed to be chased through the woods by a "green-eared spaghetti monster." Hart invites Cohle over for dinner, not realizing it is Cohle's deceased daughter's birthday.

Rust demonstrates his pessimistic philosophy, stating that "human consciousness was a tragic misstep in evolution." He is a loner — unmarried, his daughter dead, dismissive of social norms. Marty represents the conventional detective: married with two daughters, comfortable with traditional masculinity. Reverend Tuttle's political connections raise suspicion when a task force forms around the case's anti-Christian elements. A black minister mentions the police ignored vandalism and animal mutilation at his church.

Seventeen years later, Cohle and Hart are separately interviewed by Detectives Thomas Papania and Maynard Gilbough about the Dora Lange case. Hart and Cohle have not spoken in ten years after a falling-out in 2002. Cohle is shown a photograph of another girl whose body was found posed similarly to Lange, raising the question of how the killer could have struck again if he was supposedly caught in 1995. Present-day Rust appears weathered and alcoholic, while Marty seems successful running a private security firm.
